km.Slane, County Meath, Ireland: ស្លាន, កូuនtយ មែaត, អៀរឡង់
km.Athy, County Kildare, Ireland: អាធី, កូuនtយ គីលដារe, អៀរឡង់

zh.Slane, County Meath, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯斯藍
zh.Athy, County Kildare, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達阿
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard St Michael於1203年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達阿時，他的父親David de St Michael28歲，母親Margery le Fleming23歲。他於1227年與Rohesia在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1236年在他的家鄉享年33歲。

zh-hans.Slane, County Meath, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯斯藍
zh-hans.Athy, County Kildare, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達阿
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard St Michael于1203年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達阿时，他的父亲David de St Michael28岁，母亲Margery le Fleming23岁。他于1227年与Rohesia在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1236年在他的家乡享年33岁。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  French: habitational name from any of the places so called, for example in Normandy, named from a religious dedication to a (male) St. Clair.
2  North American variant spelling of   St  .   Claire  .

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

nameMeanings.name: Michael
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Jewish, and Greek: from the personal name Michael, ultimately from Hebrew Mika’el ‘Who is like God?’. This was borne by an archangel in the Bible, the protector of Israel (Daniel 10:13, 12:1; Rev. 12:7). In Christian tradition, Michael was regarded as the warrior archangel, conqueror of Satan, and the personal name was correspondingly popular throughout Europe, especially in knightly and military families. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Mihály (see Mihaly ), Czech and Slovak Michal , Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Mikhael and Mikhail , and also patronymics and other derivatives from these names, e.g. Greek Papamichael , its cognate Papamichalis, and Michaelides , Serbian Mihajlović (see Mihajlovic ), and Slovenian Mihelčič (see Mihelcic ). Compare Mikeal .

variants.name: Miceli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Vito, Mario, Giuseppe, Luciano, Rosario, Santo, Arturo, Carlo, Giuseppi, Luca, Paolo, Pasquale, Salvador, Aida, Alfredo, Augusto, Ildo, Lino, Marta.
Italian (southern): from a southern variant of the personal name Michele (see Michael ).

variants.name: Micek
meanings: Czech (Míček and Miček), Slovak (Miček), and Polish: from a pet form of personal names Michal, Michał, equivalents of Michael , or Mikuláš, Mikołaj, equivalents of Nicholas . Compare Mica .

variants.name: Mich
meanings: Polish: from a short form of any of various personal names such as Michał (see Michael ) or Mikołaj (see Nicholas ).
German (Rhineland-Palatinate): unexplained.
English: variant of   Mitch   3 or 4. This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Micale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carlo, Domenic, Salvatore, Antonio, Ferdinando, Filippo, Natale, Nicola, Orlando, Remo, Rocco, Rosario, Santo.
Italian (southern): from a southern variant of the personal name Michele (see Michael ), influenced by the Greek vernacular form Michalis.

variants.name: McElmeel
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Michil ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of Saint Michael’. The surname contains an earlier borrowing of the Biblical personal name Michael , before the lengthened vowels of the modern Irish Gaelic form Mícheál.

variants.name: McMichael
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Mhichíl, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of the personal name Michael . This was the usual shortened Anglicized form of Mac Gille Michil in Scotland.

variants.name: Mical
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Janina, Jerzy, Mieczyslaw, Zygmund.
Polish (Micał): from a vernacular form of the personal name Michał (see Michael ) or Mikołaj (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Michaeli
meanings: German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Michael  .

variants.name: Mica
meanings: Czech (mainly Míča, also Miča) and Slovak (Miča): from Mica and Miča, pet forms of the personal name Michal, an equivalent of Michael , or Mikuláš, an equivalent of Nicholas .
Romanian: from a female personal name derived from the adjective mic, feminine mică ‘small, short’.

variants.name: Mihaly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Balint, Tamas, Zoltan.
Hungarian (Mihály): from the personal name Mihály, Hungarian form of Michael .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Croatian Mihalj (see Mihal ), a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard St Michael was born in 1203, in Athy, County Kildare, Ireland, his father, David de St Michael, was 28 and his mother, Margery le Fleming, was 23. He married Rohesia in 1227, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1236, in his hometown, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard St Michael est né en 1203, à Athy, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, David de St Michael, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margery le Fleming, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Rohesia en 1227, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1236, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard St Michael 1203 in Athy, County Kildare, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater David de St Michael 28 und seine Mutter Margery le Fleming 23. Er hat 1227 in Irland Rohesia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1236 im Alter von 33 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard St Michael è nato il nel 1203 nel Athy, County Kildare, Irlanda, il suo padre, David de St Michael, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Margery le Fleming, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Rohesia nel 1227, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1236, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 33 anni.

ja.Slane, County Meath, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ メアススラン
ja.Athy, County Kildare, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ キルダアアシー
ja.lifeSummary: Richard St Michaelが1203年にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアアシーで生まれた時，父David de St Michaelは28歳，母Margery le Flemingは23歳であった。1227年にアイルランドでRohesiaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1236年に故郷で33歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard St Michael(이)가 1203년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 앳히에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David de St Michael(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery le Fleming(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1227년 아일랜드에서 Rohesia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1236년 그의 고향에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard St Michael nasceu em 1203, em Athy, County Kildare, Irlanda, seu pai, David de St Michael, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margery le Fleming, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Rohesia em 1227, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1236, em sua cidade natal, com 33 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard St Michael родился в 1203 г. в местности ати, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, его отцу по имени David de St Michael было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery le Fleming, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Rohesia в 1227 г. в местности Ирландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1236 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 33 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard St Michael nació en 1203, en Athy, County Kildare, Irlanda, su papá, David de St Michael, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margery le Fleming, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Rohesia en 1227, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1236, en su pueblo, a la edad de 33 años.
